paper_title: τ-Grounding Theorem: Physical Derivation of the Upstream Measurement Principle
author: Thomas Brennan

drp7_gate_update.note: DRP-7 gate now requires DRP-1 through DRP-6 AND DRP-8 all PHASE-READY

in_scope: DDE systems satisfying C1-C4 with measurable τ
out_of_scope: General epistemological claims not specific to DDE systems
DDE systems where τ is not independently measurable

inference_rules.name: Causal Precedence
inference_rules.logical_form: In a causal system, state at t' cannot depend on events at t when t' < t. Causal influence propagates strictly forward.
inference_rules.derives_from: C1 (DDE definition) via IR-4. Contains no §10.7 content. HR-D3: DISMISSED.

inbound_dependencies.role: UMP statement. Received as Type A axiom — conclusion only. Proof method (§10.7) is not a premise. HR-D1: DISMISSED.
Falsification Kernel K=(P,O,M,B) definition. Required for C6 vacuity condition.
β=1/2 Hopf quarter-wave theorem. Live edge for TH-1 frequency-domain derivation path.

pass_forward_register.destination_role: Type A axiom — dual-path convergence corroborates DRP-7 self-application

placeholder_register.description: Pass-forward to DRP-7 as Type A axiom pending DRP-8 PHASE-READY

principle_10_compliant_note: Set false pending CBT I-9 Step 4 audit. All entries assessed below; all LIVE_EDGE or CLOSED.

principle_10_audit.derivation_path: DRP-1 v1.1 C-DRP.8 — received as Type A axiom
DRP-1 v1.1 C-DRP.5 — received as Type A axiom
P1 v12 F-1.4 — live edge for TH-1 frequency-domain path
Accepted HR-D8-0. Derives from C1 (DDE forward evolution) via IR-4. Justified: Suppes 1970; Pearl 2009. No §10.7 content. HR-D3: DISMISSED.
Definitional. τ is the DDE delay constant defined by C1 scope conditions. Independently measurable per scope boundary.
HR-D8-3-DRS step-trace: 0/16 steps require §10.7 as necessary premise. HR-D4 and HR-D6 prose fixes applied. Independence: CONFIRMED.

claims.title: DDE Proposition
claims.statement: A proposition P is a DDE proposition if and only if: (i) P concerns the state of a system satisfying C1-C4; (ii) P's truth-maker is a state variable x(t_P) of that system at a determinate time t_P; (iii) the system's evolution is governed by a DDE with delay τ > 0, independently measurable.
claims.note: Type D — definitional. No falsification predicate required. Establishes the subject domain of the τ-Grounding Theorem. Justifies the restriction to DDE propositions in all downstream claims.

claims.title: τ-Grounded Measurement Window
claims.statement: For a DDE proposition P with truth-maker x(t_P) and system delay τ, the τ-grounded measurement window is W = {x(t_i) : t_i ≤ t_P − τ}. W is the maximal set of observations that are causally independent of x(t_P) under the forward evolution operator Φ_τ. Maximality follows from IR-3 boundary exactness: the boundary t_i = t_P − τ is inclusive and exact.
claims.note: Type D — definitional. No falsification predicate required. Established by L2 derivation chain (L2-1 through L2-4). 'Maximal' qualified by IR-3: the boundary is not a conservative approximation but the exact causal independence limit under Φ_τ.

claims.title: DDE Forward Causal Structure
claims.statement: For any DDE system satisfying C1-C4, the forward evolution operator Φ_τ is non-invertible: no observation O(t') with t' ≤ t can causally depend on x(t + τ). Backward causal paths are structurally absent.

predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: There exists a C1-C4 DDE system and a time pair (t, t') with t' ≤ t such that the measured mutual information I(O(t'); x(t + τ)) exceeds δ_causal

Φ_τ.definition: Forward evolution map of the C1-C4 DDE: x(t + τ) = Φ_τ[x(t), history on [t − τ, t]]

O(t').definition: Set of system state observations recorded at time t'

x(t + τ).definition: System state at time t + τ, determined by Φ_τ acting on history

I(·;·).definition: Shannon mutual information between observation set and future state, computed by standard estimator

δ_causal.definition: Causal independence threshold: maximum mutual information attributable to measurement noise and finite-sample estimator variance, determined from baseline measurement at system equilibrium

predicate.EVALUATION: 1. Select C1-C4 system with independently measured τ. 2. Record observations O(t') at time t' ≤ t. 3. Record x(t + τ) at time t + τ. 4. Compute I(O(t'); x(t + τ)) using standard mutual information estimator. 5. Determine δ_causal from equilibrium baseline. 6. If I > δ_causal: FALSIFIED. If I ≤ δ_causal for all tested pairs: NOT FALSIFIED. Procedure terminates in finite steps.
predicate.BOUNDARY: δ_causal is an exclusive upper bound. I > δ_causal (strictly greater) constitutes falsification. δ_causal is substrate-specific and must be determined from equilibrium baseline prior to evaluation. Justified: causal independence admits measurement noise; the threshold accounts for finite-sample mutual information estimator variance and must not be set to zero.
predicate.CONTEXT: C1-C4 DDE systems with measurable τ > 0. Sub-critical (μ < 0). Systems where τ is not independently measurable are out of scope per D8-0.1.

claims.sample_falsification_observation: A C1-C4 delayed feedback network where upstream observations at t' < t_P − τ show I > δ_causal with the future state x(t_P + τ), indicating backward causal coupling. This would require Φ_τ to be invertible, contradicting the DDE forward evolution structure.
hostile_review_notes: HR-D8 (HR-D8-3-DRS): vacuity witness uses backward-coupled noise injection violating C1. DISMISSED — witness is the counterexample demonstrating predicate discriminating power; it need not satisfy C1-C4 well-behavedness. C6: PASS.

claims.title: τ-Gap Measurement Window
claims.statement: For a DDE proposition P with truth-maker x(t_P) and delay τ, the maximal causally independent observation window is W = {x(t_i) : t_i ≤ t_P − τ}. Observations from {t_i > t_P − τ} may be causally downstream of x(t_P) and cannot be guaranteed causally independent.

predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: There exists a C1-C4 DDE proposition P and a set of observations {x(t_j)} with t_j ∈ (t_P − τ, t_P] such that I(x(t_j); x(t_P)) ≤ δ_causal for all t_j in this interval — i.e., observations outside W are empirically indistinguishable from causally independent observations

P.definition: DDE proposition per C-DRP8.D1 with truth-maker x(t_P)

t_P.definition: Time at which truth-maker x(t_P) is determined

τ.definition: DDE delay, independently measured per C1-C4 scope conditions. Not inferred from P.

t_j.definition: Observation time constrained to t_j ∈ (t_P − τ, t_P]

W.definition: τ-grounded measurement window per C-DRP8.D2: W = {x(t_i) : t_i ≤ t_P − τ}

I(·;·).definition: Shannon mutual information, computed by standard estimator

δ_causal.definition: Causal independence threshold as defined in C-DRP8.1

predicate.EVALUATION: 1. Select C1-C4 DDE system with independently measured τ. 2. Identify proposition P with truth-maker x(t_P). 3. Select observations x(t_j) with t_j ∈ (t_P − τ, t_P]. 4. Compute I(x(t_j); x(t_P)) for each t_j. 5. If I ≤ δ_causal for all t_j in this interval: FALSIFIED (the W boundary is over-restrictive; observations outside W are also causally independent, contradicting the claim). If I > δ_causal for at least one t_j: NOT FALSIFIED. Procedure terminates in finite steps.
predicate.BOUNDARY: Boundary at t_i = t_P − τ is exact and inclusive (IR-3). Observations at t_i = t_P − τ are included in W. The boundary is determined by τ, not by measurement resolution. Justified: Φ_τ's domain is history on [t − τ, t]; the causal independence boundary is a consequence of the operator's definition, not a conservative approximation.
predicate.CONTEXT: C1-C4 DDE systems with measurable τ. τ must be independently measured and must not be inferred from the proposition P being evaluated. Inference of τ from P would violate UMP (C-DRP.8).

inbound_axiom_receipts: C-DRP.8 received at L2-0 as Type A axiom — conclusion only, not §10.7 proof method. Axiom receipt does not constitute dependence on proof method. HR-D1: DISMISSED.
claims.sample_falsification_observation: A DDE oscillator where observations at t_j ∈ (t_P − τ, t_P) show mutual information with x(t_P) indistinguishable from equilibrium baseline, demonstrating that the W boundary is over-restrictive and the causal window extends further than τ predicts.

claims.title: UMP Necessity for DDE Propositions
claims.statement: For any DDE proposition P satisfying C1-C4 and C6 (non-vacuity), the Falsification Kernel K=(P,O,M,B) is non-vacuous only if O ⊆ W, where W is the τ-grounded measurement window. Any K with O ⊄ W is vacuous for DDE propositions: M cannot distinguish P-true from P-false states because observations within the causal window of x(t_P) are dynamically consistent with both truth values.

predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: There exists a C1-C4 DDE proposition P and a Falsification Kernel K=(P,O,M,B) with O ⊄ W such that M(O) reliably distinguishes P-true from P-false states — i.e., M's output differs between P-true and P-false presentations in a statistically significant and repeatable manner

O.definition: Set of observations supplied to M for evaluation

M.definition: Measurement procedure operating on O, returning FALSIFIED / NOT FALSIFIED / INDETERMINATE

vacuity.definition: K is vacuous if M returns NOT FALSIFIED under all states consistent with P regardless of P's truth value. Per C6 from C-DRP.5.

O ⊄ W.definition: O contains at least one observation x(t_j) with t_j > t_P − τ

predicate.EVALUATION: 1. Construct C1-C4 DDE system. 2. Define DDE proposition P with truth-maker x(t_P). 3. Construct K with O ⊄ W (at least one observation at t_j > t_P − τ). 4. Present system in P-true state; run M(O); record output. 5. Present system in P-false state; run M(O); record output. 6. Repeat across N trials. 7. If M(O) output differs reliably between P-true and P-false states (statistical test at α = 0.05, N ≥ 30): FALSIFIED. If M(O) output is statistically indistinguishable: NOT FALSIFIED. Procedure terminates in finite steps.
predicate.BOUNDARY: O ⊄ W is a sufficient condition for vacuity in DDE propositions — at least one observation outside W suffices. The claim does not assert that O ⊆ W guarantees non-vacuity (other conditions may also be required). The mechanism is physical: observations within the causal window of x(t_P) are dynamically consistent with both truth values under the DDE's forward evolution (L3-2), making discrimination impossible regardless of M's design.
predicate.CONTEXT: C1-C4 DDE systems with measurable τ. C6 (non-vacuity requirement) from C-DRP.5 is a live inbound edge. Scope is DDE propositions only. General epistemological claims and non-DDE systems are out of scope per D8-0.1.

inbound_axiom_receipts: C-DRP.8 received at L3-0 as Type A axiom — conclusion only. HR-D1: DISMISSED.
C-DRP.5 K structure required for C6 vacuity definition.
HR-D4 fix: C-DRP.8 reference at L3-4 moved to notes field — convergence note only, not premise. L3-4 follows from L3-3 + C6 alone. Independence preserved.
claims.sample_falsification_observation: A DDE proposition evaluated with observations drawn from within the causal window of x(t_P) — t_j ∈ (t_P − τ, t_P) — where M's output reliably tracks P's truth value across repeated trials. This would demonstrate that observations outside W can discriminate, falsifying the necessity claim.
hostile_review_notes: HR-D5 (HR-D8-3-DRS): L3-3 does not address model-based inference. DISMISSED — K structure (C-DRP.5) defines M as operating on O (measurement procedure, not simulator). Model-based inference from observations in the causal window cannot disambiguate P because those observations are dynamically consistent with both truth values (L3-2).

claims.title: Dual-Path Convergence: τ as Common Cause of β=1/2 and UMP Necessity
claims.statement: The delay parameter τ is the common physical cause of two independently derived results: (i) β=1/2, established by TH-1 via the frequency-domain characteristic equation of the DDE at Hopf criticality (receiving F-1.4 as live edge); and (ii) UMP necessity for DDE propositions, established by TH-2 via the causal graph of Φ_τ and the L1-L3 derivation chain. The two paths share no domain-specific intermediate steps and converge on τ as their common structural constraint. This constitutes structural, not analogical, convergence.

predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: For any step S_k in derivation D8-2.0 (steps L1-0 through TH-CON): S_k logically requires, as a necessary premise, a claim originating from DRP-1 §10.7's contradiction proof — where logical necessity means S_k cannot be derived from its remaining premises and inference rules without that claim

S_k.definition: Any of the 16 indexed steps in D8-2.0: L1-0 through TH-CON

D8-2.0.definition: The complete Phase 2 derivation table for DRP-8, a fixed artifact produced at S54 prior to PHASE-READY evaluation

necessary_premise.definition: Claim X is a necessary premise of step S_k if removing X from S_k's justification set renders S_k underivable from remaining premises and inference rules. Logical necessity, not genetic necessity.

DRP-1_§10.7.definition: The contradiction proof in DRP-1 establishing UMP by assuming O=f(P) and deriving contradiction with non-vacuity requirement C6

independence.definition: No step in the TH-2 derivation path (L1-0 through TH-2) has §10.7 content as a logically necessary premise

predicate.EVALUATION: 1. Obtain D8-2.0 derivation record (fixed artifact). 2. For each step S_k (16 steps), extract justification field and inference rules cited. 3. Determine whether §10.7 contradiction argument appears as necessary (not merely confirming): attempt re-derivation of S_k without §10.7. 4. If S_k cannot be re-derived: FALSIFIED. 5. If all 16 steps are re-derivable without §10.7: NOT FALSIFIED. Step-trace is finite (16 steps). Procedure terminates. Current result: HR-D8-3-DRS step-trace found 0/16 steps requiring §10.7. Status: NOT FALSIFIED.
predicate.BOUNDARY: (a) Necessary premise excludes: sharing common axioms (C1-C4 scope, IR-1 Modus Ponens), receiving C-DRP.8 as Type A axiom conclusion. (b) Axiom receipt at L2-0 and L3-0 is not dependence on §10.7 proof method — the axiom's justification is external to derivations that use it. HR-D1: DISMISSED. (c) Structural similarity of conclusions (both paths reach UMP necessity) does not constitute dependence. Convergence requires same conclusion from different premises. HR-D2: DISMISSED. (d) TH-3 qualification (HR-D6 fix): TH-1 and TH-2 share no domain-specific intermediate steps. Both receive C1 as common axiom but proceed through disjoint technical machinery: TH-1 via characteristic equation at Hopf criticality; TH-2 via causal graph of Φ_τ. No domain-specific step from either path appears in the other.
predicate.CONTEXT: Scope: DDE systems satisfying C1-C4. The convergence claim is structural: τ appears as a necessary parameter in both derivation paths. 'Common cause' means structural necessity, not analogical resemblance or isomorphism.

claims.independence_basis: HR-D8-3-DRS PASS. Step-trace of all 16 derivation steps: 0/16 require §10.7 as necessary premise.
claims.sample_falsification_observation: Identification of a step in D8-2.0 where removing the §10.7 contradiction argument leaves the step underivable from remaining premises and inference rules. For example: if L3-2's dynamic consistency argument required §10.7's epistemological framing as a necessary premise, removing §10.7 would leave L3-2 unsupported. HR-D8-3-DRS found no such step across all 16.

HR-D6: TH-3 statement qualified to 'no domain-specific intermediate steps beyond common axioms and standard inference rules.'
